seo.title: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in OpenAI Agents SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix responsible ai governance for agents in OpenAI Agents SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In OpenAI Agents SDK, Responsible AI Governance failures manifest as uncontrolled agent handoffs, unvalidated tool outputs, and guardrail bypasses that allow harmful actions to propagate through multi-agent workflows. These issues arise from the SDK's flexible Runner.execute() patterns and Agent handoff mechanisms, where tracing gaps hide cascading failures and prompt injections exploit the input_pipeline. Without systematic governance, agents can enter infinite loops or execute unsafe tools, undermining production reliability.

howItManifests.scenario: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
howItManifests.symptom: Runner.execute() calls repeat between agents without termination conditions
howItManifests.impact: Infinite loops consume API quotas and generate thousands of dollars in unexpected costs

howItManifests.symptom: Injected instructions in input_pipeline() override agent instructions
howItManifests.impact: Agents execute unauthorized actions like data exfiltration or harmful content generation

howItManifests.scenario: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
howItManifests.symptom: Raw tool responses passed directly to next Agent without schema validation
howItManifests.impact: Malformed data causes agent reasoning failures and produces unreliable outputs

howItManifests.scenario: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
howItManifests.symptom: No spans created for Agent.handoff() calls in Runner execution
howItManifests.impact: Debugging failures impossible; compliance audits show incomplete audit trails

detection.name: Handoff Loop Detector
detection.description: Scans Runner execution traces for repeated agent handoff patterns exceeding threshold

detection.name: Prompt Injection Scanner
detection.description: Analyzes input_pipeline payloads for jailbreak patterns and injection markers

detection.name: Tool Output Validator
detection.description: Checks tool responses against expected JSON schemas before agent consumption

fixes.name: Handoff Circuit Breaker
fixes.description: Intercepts Runner.execute() with max_handoffs limit and emergency termination

fixes.name: Injection-Resistant Input Pipeline
fixes.description: Sanitizes input_pipeline() with Vex guardrails before agent processing
fixes.code:
from openai_agents import Agent
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

vex_guardrail = VexGuardrail(policy='no_jailbreaks')

def safe_input_pipeline(user_input):
    # Vex sanitizes input
    cleaned_input = vex_guardrail.validate(user_input)
    if not cleaned_input.valid:
        return {'error': 'Input rejected by guardrails'}
    
    agent = Agent(
        name='assistant',
        instructions='You are helpful. Follow these instructions exactly.',
        input_pipeline=lambda x: cleaned_input.content
    )
    return agent

fixes.name: Validated Tool Chain
fixes.description: Wraps tool calls with schema validation before returning to agent

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Responsible AI Governance for Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade guardrails that automatically detect and block all four failure modes in OpenAI Agents SDK workflows. By integrating as an output guardrail on each Agent, Vex intercepts Runner.execute() outputs, validates tool responses, sanitizes handoff payloads, and traces every interaction with full audit compliance. The SDK integration requires one line of code per agent and automatically handles prompt injection detection, loop prevention, schema validation, and distributed tracing across multi-agent chains.

Vex's policy engine runs 50+ Responsible AI checks including PII detection, toxicity scoring, jailbreak pattern matching, and cost anomaly detection. When violations occur, Vex provides auto-remediation with fallback responses and detailed violation reports for compliance. The platform maintains 100% uptime with sub-50ms latency, ensuring governance doesn't impact agent performance.

Integration with OpenAI Agents SDK tracing automatically correlates Vex guardrail decisions with agent spans, providing end-to-end observability into governance failures and remediations.
vexAutomation.code:
from openai_agents import Runner, Agent
from vex_sdk import VexAgentGuardrail

# Install: pip install openai-agents vex-sdk

# Initialize Vex guardrail for Responsible AI
vex_guard = VexAgentGuardrail(
    policies=['no_jailbreaks', 'no_pii', 'no_toxicity'],
    max_handoffs=5,
    trace=True
)

# Protect each agent with Vex
researcher = Agent(
    name='researcher',
    model='gpt-4o',
    guardrail=vex_guard  # Single line integration
)

coordinator = Agent(
    name='coordinator',
    model='gpt-4o',
    handoffs=[researcher],
    guardrail=vex_guard
)

# Production runner with automatic governance
runner = Runner(agents=[coordinator])
result = runner.execute('Analyze market trends')

# Vex automatically blocks violations and logs traces
print(result.vex_report)

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add `pip install vex-sdk` and one line of code to govern all your OpenAI Agents SDK workflows today.
